ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-29-2020 Off Calendar

ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-29-2020 Continued To Jun-04-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503

ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-05-2020 Continued To May-07-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503

Notice Of Motion For Order To Apportion And Distribute Funds
Off Calendar Jan-30-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Motion to Apportion and Distribute Settlement Funds.
Dept. 514. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Michael Shintaku, CSR #8251. Telephone: 408410-8040. Email: emailformike@hotmail.com.

Kimberly J. Chu, Esq. appeared with Wrongful Death Heir Queen T'Hisha, who was placed under oath and testified.
Ms. T'Hisha appeared for this morning's proceedings via CourtCall.
No appearance by wrongful death heir Gregory Miller Randall.

The Court, having reviewed the moving papers and having considered the evidence, both oral and documentary, and good cause appearing, RULES that the following are entitled to distribution of all net funds from settlements from the underlying survival/wrongful death claims, in the following proportions:

Estate of Jessie Randall, deceased - 100%.
Queen T'Hisha as Wrongful Death Heir - 0%.
Gregory Miller Randall as Wrongful Death Heir - 0%.

Additionally, funds shall be issued by Brayton Purcell LLP in the percentages set forth above, subject first to fees and costs, (if any outstanding) and liens, including but not limited to liens (if any) payable to the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services' Medicare program and the California Department of Health Care Services Medi-Cal Program, and subject to any holds placed on funds awaiting response from such agencies.

Furthermore, all future net funds derived from settlements entered after the October 13, 2011 death of Jessie Randall shall be distributed in the percentages set forth above.

Lastly, the signature of Queen T'Hisha shall be sufficient on all settlement documents for the purpose of settling wrongful death claims with defendants in the wrongful death action and in those claims made against bankruptcy-related trusts established for asbestos victims.

Proposed Order to Apportion and Distribute Funds was submitted to and signed by the Court.
